old_claim.note: Telegraph provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from magnetic_tape_data_storage to telegraph. Digital tape systems are modeled as post-telegraph digital signal recording components, not telegraph derivatives.

source_shape.source_locator: CHM entry and timeline identify UNISERVO/IBM 726 as computer storage hardware introduced after earlier telegraph systems.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source gives a product-level history of magnetic tape drives in early digital computers, without describing telegraph as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The telegraph is not a direct prerequisite for the digital magnetic tape storage node; the node is scoped to post-telegraph computer storage architecture.

ontology_before: The graph linked telegraph as an enabling technological predecessor of magnetic_tape_data_storage.
ontology_after: Telegraph is removed from direct dependency since the node now represents early computer storage hardware rather than telegraph communication systems.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: removed the magnetic_tape_data_storage -> telegraph edge; direct dependencies are now restricted to enabling electronics and materials/chemistry.
would_reject_if: The scoped node were redefined as a telegraph-adjacent communications mechanism.
A source directly demonstrated a required telegraph technology chain into digital magnetic tape storage.
The direct edge to telegraph were reintroduced without accompanying scope changes.
short_reason: Telegraph systems are unrelated to the computer-storage architecture introduced with UNIVAC and IBM magnetic tape.
